programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part seven of eight. Tension mounts at the start of the trial of Fox and Morientes for the murder of Carly Samuels and Grace is shocked to encounter an old flame in the courtroom. Smithy is furious to learn a key witness has retracted his statement and sets out with Emma to persuade Alisha Danniels to testify instead. Back at the station, a prisoner smashes his head against the wall and claims Nate attacked him

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden breaks up with Faye, but when she is threatened by a drug addict, his chivalrous attitude ends up placing him in grave danger. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy is elated when he visits his father and they begin to settle their differences. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Concluding the eight-part story. Smithy finally gets his witness to court to testify against Fox and Morientes after Tony rescues the crash victims from the overturned car. Defence barrister Sunil Davdra pulls Alisha's testimony apart but the case takes another surprising turn before a verdict is reached. On the Jasmine Allen estate, Beth and Reg find themselves barricaded in the mobile police unit as rioting breaks out

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan presses Frankie for an answer, Lorraine despairs at David's parenting skills, Joe makes a dramatic announcement, and Grant attempts a showdown with David

programme.desc: Jeremy announces he is planning to marry his girlfriend, but is surprised at his friends' response. Meanwhile, Cate Hobbs receives bad news

programme.desc: Tristan has a mishap while treating a cow, leaving the Darrowby practice facing a possible lawsuit. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Eric gets involved with an Irish rock band, while Lovejoy takes to the water with Lady Jane's friend Victoria Cavero and bravely pops the question - only to receive an unexpected response. Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton and Chris Jury star with guests Joanna Lumley and the members of Hothouse Flowers

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: The further romantic misadventures of the charming painter, played by Karl Howman. Jacko's interest in football is revived - much to Eric and Jean's amusement

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The store advertises on Citizen Band radio, but unfortunately Mrs Slocombe's attempt to publicise her underwear attracts the attentions of the wrong type of customer. Shop-floor comedy, with Mollie Sugden and featuring the voice of Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour departs for a new teaching job, leaving Clegg and Compo at a loose end now he is no longer around to drag them into his eccentric schemes. However, they do not realise an old friend is about to return to the village. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy makes an ill-advised attempt to establish long-distance communication with Compo and Clegg by investing in some walkie-talkies. However, it doesn't take long for one of the units to fall foul of a tractor. Long-running rural comedy, with Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The detective is called out when a body is found on a rubbish pile under the Tyne Bridge. It initially looks like suicide, but evidence reveals the victim was dead before he landed. The search begins to uncover his identity, with just a pawnbroker's receipt, a note with a phone number for one Gwendolyn Howard and a betting slip in the deceased's pocket. Crime drama, guest starring Jim Moir (aka Vic Reeves)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After Sandra's departure, the detectives are anxious to meet their new boss, and DCI Sasha Miller wastes no time getting cracking with her first Ucos case, the murder of an Italian immigrant on a west London allotment 25 years earlier. The ritualistic style of the killing puts one of the other gardeners in the frame when it's revealed he is a practising druid. However, as the investigation continues, Gerry becomes suspicious of Sasha - why is she kissing a senior officer? Tamzin Outhwaite joins the cast as DCI Miller, with Dennis Waterman, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo visits old college friend Sean Delaney at his psychiatric care centre in Essex, where one of the patients has been found dead in her bed. Although the coroner ruled a verdict of sudden-death syndrome, Delaney is not convinced and asks Leo to investigate. Suspicion soon falls on the dead girl's father, who has lost the family fortune, and his wife - but the pathologist runs into problems when the local detective sergeant makes it clear she is suspicious of his motives. Adrian Dunbar, Tamzin Outhwaite and Tom Ellis guest star

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Penny is heartbroken when Hugh makes a series of disastrous choices about his marriage and career, while love is in the air on Hayley and Ajax's wedding day

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: After Antietam, the Greens suffer a setback and Pinkerton's inquiry heats up. Foster and Samuel visit Foster's family plantation, where they intervene in a difficult labour. The hospital staff unites to oust their hated chief. In Boston, Mary's condition worsens

programme.desc: The further romantic misadventures of the charming painter, played by Karl Howman. Jacko's interest in football is revived - much to Eric and Jean's amusement

Meanwhile, a grieving Faye lies to Joseph, telling him Linden is the father of her unborn child, and Michael tries to make use of the HolbyCare scheme to reinvent himself as the hospital's saviour. Guy Henry, who appears in the final two Harry Potter films, joins the cast

programme.desc: In the concluding part of this crossover between the hospital dramas, the explosion rips through Nightingale Wing and a small band of survivors are trapped above the third floor as the fire rages around them. A rescue operation is mounted straight away - but it seems unlikely that all will escape with their lives

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following a string of humiliating set-ups by teenagers, Gina clamps down on hoax calls and assigns Will to screen all incoming messages, but the plaintive voice of a youngster claiming he and his brother have been kidnapped by their father convinces Nikki it's the real thing. Confronting the suicidal parent (guest star Ralph Ineson) at the docks, she persuades him to release the children - but only on condition she takes their place in his car

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lorraine confronts Grant about David's flat. Martin gets into a fight at school over Mark being HIV positive. Kathy tries to make Ian look after Lucy

programme.desc: It's bonfire night and Joe tries to set fire to himself. Carol and David are sleeping together. Peggy finds a lump and is advised to go to a doctor

A truck carrying men and weapons to Aldershot is ambushed by hooded mercenaries and Chris' intervention leads to him being shot and taken hostage

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried deals with a miserly scrap merchant notorious for mistreating his horses and comes up trumps. Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lady Jane persuades Lovejoy to act as driver for an old friend who has to attend her sea-faring brother's burial. The roguish antiques dealer is given the job of selling the late captain's Second World War medals, and there is a smart fashion photographer who wants them. However, some drunken sailors then come into the picture and Lovejoy finds himself dealing with the gun-toting Italian Angelo Pantaloni. Light-hearted drama, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko goes on his first real date with Lucia, and is shocked to discover she is not as timid as he thought. Elmo turns to Lesley for help in giving his wine bar a touch of class

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The gang visits 10 Downing Street to campaign against a Japanese takeover - and Mrs Slocombe gets a chance to demonstrate that Britain has women even more formidable than Mrs Thatcher. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo, Clegg and Foggy are intrigued by some perfectly respectable men who turn up without their trousers, before they take part in an event sponsored by a village charity

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Feeling frustrated by his lack of fame, Foggy invents the sport of bicycle polo, convinced it will not only put his name in lights but prove a lucrative venture. Long-running comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Newly demoted Havers joins Lynley on a double murder investigation. After a girl is killed and her camping companion stabbed, two separate lines of inquiry lead the duo into a murky world of deception, corruption and prostitution in their bid to discover the murderer's identity. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small, Timothy West and Simon Williams

programme.desc: The detectives are asked to carry out a preliminary investigation into a corrupt senior officer who may have suppressed evidence when a notorious crime boss was put away for murdering a prominent doctor. Meanwhile, Steve gets a shock when his estranged son turns up at work to see him. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star

programme.desc: An ageing confectionery company owner is found dead in his London hotel suite, and while Nikki tries to convince the sceptical DS Gold he was murdered, several businessmen begin to circle as it turns out the dead man was losing a desperate fight to prevent the sale of his company to investors. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves, with guest appearances by Tim Pigott-Smith, Sharon Small and Priyanga Burford

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: After Lucia tricks him into proposing, Jacko needs to come up with a desperate plan to get out of the wedding - but realises he may simply have to tell her parents the truth

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe's beloved cat goes missing, so she turns to Mr Humphries for support in her hour of need. Fearing the feline may never return, the staff buy her a kitten. John Inman and Mollie Sugden star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo seizes the opportunity to break into the world of showbusiness when human cannonball Captain Zero cannot perform his act at the local garden fete. Trevor Peacock (The Vicar of Dibley) guest stars, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy plans to convert an old boat into a water taxi, but when work is finally completed Compo becomes obsessed with getting Nora Batty on board

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Everard hands down a lenient sentence to a political benefactor accused of running over a mother and her two children. Deed accuses him of corruption, and Monty responds by putting pressure on Jo, ostracising her to the point where she begs her friend to back down. True to form, John continues to push for the result he wants a tougher sentence but there may be a price for him to pay

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back home in Bermondsey for his youngest daughter's wedding, Gerry is visited by Ralph Paxton, an old friend recently released from prison who wants him investigate the murder of his grandson. Meanwhile, a newly divorced Sasha is forced to work with ex-husband Ned Hancock, Danny puts the body language skills he has acquired on an FBI course to the test, and Gerry's daughter Caitlin gets upset when her father interrogates her fiance about his credentials. Cold case drama, starring Dennis Waterman, Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst, with a guest appearance by David Hayman (Trial & Retribution)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team is baffled by the conflicting possible motives after two women are shot dead in a house basement - and the investigation isn't helped by Jack having to work alongside an old flame, the ambitious DI Chrissy Reed. Meanwhile, Leo faces a difficult choice when he is asked to prove his pathologist mentor made a mistake in a case that resulted in a mother being imprisoned for smothering her baby to death. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally and Ben are shocked to find a 12-year-old at the wheel of a stolen vehicle belonging to known violent offender Dylan Morrison. Taking the youngster home, they discover his father has cause to fear Morrison, and explicit pictures of a minor celebrity found in the car indicate the reason for the bitter hatred at the heart of the case. Paul Copley guest stars

programme.desc: Belgium v Wales (Kick-off 7.45pm). Coverage of both teams' opening 2022 FIFA World Cup Group E qualifier at Den Dreef in Leuven. With Dylan Ebenezer, Owain Tudur Jones, Gwennan Harries and John Hartson

programme.title: Cofio Dai Davies
programme.desc: A portrait of one of Welsh football's most recognisable faces, the former goalkeeper, Dai Davies, who won 52 caps for Wales while playing for Everton, Wrexham and Swansea. Neville Southall, Gareth Edwards and Dixie McNeil are among those paying tribute to the giant from the Amman Valley

programme.title: Pum Merch, Tri Chopa, Un Cwch
programme.desc: After the crew of the yacht Aparito Digital contend with the sailing leg from Caernarfon to Whitehaven, runners Lowri Morgan and Jo Jackson face Scafell Pike in stormy conditions

programme.title: Sgwrs Dan y Lloer
programme.desc: A conversation with the Senedd's Presiding Officer Elin Jones, one of the most influential women in the history of Welsh devolution. She talks about her childhood in the Llanwnen area, her days singing with Cwlwm, and what fuels her politics.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Former England star Paul Stewart reflects on the abuse of young boys by predatory football coaches. Magazine show, hosted by Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a garage in Margate, Kent, a ground-floor in London's Shepherd's Bush and a piece of land in Birmingham

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley are live, asking for your help with unsolved cases

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a patient in the Glasgow Airport operations tower with a history of heart attacks, and a trauma team arrive by helicopter at a high speed car crash in Forth Valley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Newark, Nottinghamshire, with experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt. Featuring a look at the profitable field of police memorabilia

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma and Zara host a menopausal support group and learn as much about their own experiences and friendship as the other women

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from London find a new home in Wiltshire with room for some animals. She also transforms a fossil into a piece of stylish jewellery

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang prepare meals against the clock, using ingredients brought in by two friends who teach young people life skills. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team set to work restoring a precious Indian stringed instrument, a Second World War pilot's jacket and a broken toy bird that has lost its tweet

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the first UK lockdown, Jane Corbin investigates Covid-19 policies pursued by governments around the world, and sets out to discover which nations have had the most successful approach, how the outcomes can be analysed, and attempts to understand what lessons might be learned for future pandemics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick and Linda are finally back behind the bar at the Vic, and soon receive a visit from DI Pine with the news that Katy has been arrested. But when Stuart listens in, he is desperate to get revenge for his old friend. Stacey gets bad news from her solicitor, prompting Jean into an angry confrontation with Jean and Martin. Kheerat and Suki are unhappy when Vinny gives a statement to the police, and Tiffany accidentally overcharges a customer, giving Dotty a money-making idea

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Penny-pinching Basil goes behind Sybil's back to save money on structural alterations to the hotel and hires an incompetent cowboy builder to do the job - an underhand tactic that ends up costing him dearly. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Three-part documentary exploring historic child abuse that took place in youth football in England from the 1970s to the 1990s and the code of silence surrounding it. The first episode focuses on former footballer Andy Woodward's 2016 revelation of abuse at the hands of a youth coach, and the personal accounts of players from various youth sides

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The grand finale of the drag queen competition has arrived, with choreographer on hand to coach the finalists an through all-singing all-dancing extravaganza to impress judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Graham Norton. All of this year's eliminated queens will be returning to the studio to enjoy the show and see this year's superstar crowned

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties in Bigrigg in Cumbria, Church Gresley in Derbyshire and Hammersmith in west London

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A trauma team arrives by helicopter at a high-speed car crash in Forth Valley, while John visits a Hugh Jackman super-fan who needs help with her breathing

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel faces a race against time to get to the bottom of the dodgy dealings going on at the university. Al discovers the true identity of his online chess opponent and Rob is caught between two brothers as a case of extortion explodes into violence

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired couple spend £1million on a character property in the Welsh Borders, trying to find somewhere with holiday let potential or space for a glamping business. Away from the search, Jules also meets experts in traditional construction methods who are keeping heritage building skills alive

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Best friends Sandra and Rachel have recently embarked on a weight loss and healthier lifestyle plan, and are hoping to get some new ideas to bring fantastic flavour to ingredients they know well. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as they bring some some friendly rivalry to the kitchen, with help from chefs Romy Gill and Anna Haugh

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Lockdown Live: What Next?
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Nick Robinson present this live BBC News special, one year on since the UK's first lockdown. Broadcasting from a mass vaccination centre in Newcastle, they'll be joined by a panel of special guests who have stories to tell about how the Covid-19 pandemic has impacted everyone. Naga and Nick reflect on some of the biggest lockdown stories of the year, and look forward to ask how the world is going to change beyond the pandemic

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The news of Katy's arrest becomes common knowledge and Mick fears that everyone knows it is about him. Meanwhile, Frankie meets Stuart at the car lot office. Ruby makes her feelings about the Slaters pretty clear to Kush, while Jean comes up with a plan to save Stacey. Racist graffiti on the Minute Mart brings up painful memories for the Panesars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Two members of staff find themselves in a tight spot, as Louis is bleeding out after being attacked by an old friend, while Cameron still has Ange held hostage - although the truth of his crimes is coming to light. Can he lie his way out of Holby or will those he has tricked manage to save their colleague end his reign of terror? Meanwhile, Dom returns to work after his operation and his decision to sue the hospital - and despite his attempts to forget the past, a petty patient soon revives his hunger for revenge

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the last of the heats which sees five more hopefuls competing for an apron by cooking their signature dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. The two best will automatically go through, while the remaining three must cook two courses for John and Gregg as well as Dhruv Baker and Alex Rushmer - champion and finalist from the 2010 competition

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary explores why it has taken so long for the full scale of child abuse in football to emerge. The documentary follows the investigation into former Southampton youth coach Bob Higgins, filming alongside Hampshire Police detectives as they conduct interviews with Higgins and the survivors

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Sheriff removes Zach Shelby's body from Norma's room. She is certain that it is Jake Abernathy's doing, and when flowers arrive anonymously she interprets the gesture as a veiled threat. The sadistic mother decides that buying the motel was a mistake and makes plans to move out, but her son makes clear his objections

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: With Jake Abernathy expecting her to meet him at midnight with the $150,000, Norma goes to Sheriff Romero, who assures her that he will take care of the situation and that she has nothing to worry about. Not quite assured, she asks Dylan to get her a gun and show her how to use it. Meanwhile, Romero visits Keith Summers' sister Maggie and learns Jake's real name

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in the Devon town of Seaton, Crewe in Cheshire and London's South Norwood

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Pete and Gary are called to a patient in Paisley who has fallen down the stairs, and quickly realise he needs more support than just medical care. Martyn and Kevin treat an elderly patient who has fallen on an icy pedestrianised street

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley offer expert advice at Southwell racecourse, Nottinghamshire, as two good friends take on a father and son-in-law. Anita Manning hosts

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid receives a challenge from an old friend and Valerie gets a mysterious letter. Luca deals with a family whose need for travel vaccinations leads to trouble

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Peak District with a couple returning from the Netherlands, who want their generous £1.5million budget to provide accommodation for their visiting mothers

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband and wife Raymond and Karen are both vegan but are bored of making the same old thing. Paired with chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, they hope to come away with some new dishes for their repertoire. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Furniture restorer Will Kirk is presented with a damaged mother of pearl table, badly burned after a hot pan was mistakenly placed on its surface. And mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a broken toy aeroplane that doubles up as a memento of a lost childhood in Iraq

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Caitlin is now pregnant with her boss Kate's baby, and they navigate the complex relationship between a surrogate and intended parent, while also working together every day. Gay couple Alex and Richard begin the awkward process of home inseminations with their surrogate, Maddie, while single man David and surrogate Lauren end their relationship

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss offer advice as contestants head out into the pouring rain in Shrewsbury to secure items of potential profit under the gavel, where auctioneer Philip Serrell awaits. Love is in the air for presenter Christina Trevanion as she explains how to flirt using a fan

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Married couple Marcus and Paul are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Anna Haugh in the fast-and-furious cookery contest. Paul does most of the cooking in their home and he's keen for Marcus to get more involved. The winner will be decided in a hectic 10-Minute Challenge where the chefs must cook against the clock to produce show stopping dishes under pressure. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Kenilworth Castle, near Coventry. Intriguing treasures brought in for valuation include a miniature safe, one of the world's first interactive golf games, and a trilby belonging to an 1980s pop star. Fiona also delves into the story of a car industry entrepreneur who saved the estate from ruin

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Motivational Monday, with Gethin Jones

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart investigates the powerful natural forces that shaped the planet over billions of years. Starting with volcanos, he abseils to the edge of an Ethiopian lava lake and explores the underwater caverns believed to have been created by the meteor that wiped out the dinosaurs. The presenter also explains the positive role played by the greenhouse effect many millennia ago in ending the last ice age

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss continue their rivalry at an antiques fair in Peterborough, buying and selling collectibles for the most profit

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss compete over four very different locations to find the best antiques to turn a profit on. It's a tense finale with both investing a lot of money in their final lots as they approach the showdown auction

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for party food, including Lorraine Pascale's white chocolate lollipops, seafood dishes by James Martin and Rachel Khoo's coq-au-vin on a stick

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the anatomy and physiology of the largest land animal on the planet, the elephant. Its size seems ill-suited to surviving the most arid regions of Africa, but its inner workings allow it to defy the extreme heat of the desert, and find food and water in seemingly barren landscapes, while its extraordinary memory enables it to repel predators. Here, Chris reveals how hairs on the skin help to keep the elephant cool, and how sensors in its feet may be able to guide it toward rain

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Morecambe, Lancashire, antiques experts Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon find treasures to take to auction including a vintage Rolex watch. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers the rags to riches story of the town's most famous son - Eric Morecambe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Following Jez and Ieuan, two young men from Llanelli who had a dream to change their lives and become pizza-makers extraordinaire. The pair decided to buy an old Italian van, put an oven in the back and set off on an adventure to enter the World Pizza Championships in Parma. In the first episode, the pair get some last minute tips from their favourite Cardiff pizzeria, host their own special send-off before packing up their van, Smokey Pete, and hitting the road

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge continues to celebrate the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to easy-to-make crowd-pleasers and family classics. In this edition, Mary focuses on food that exemplifies indulgence, demonstrating mouth-watering dishes that give the mundane a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as she investigates the history of the British pie and reveals the secrets to making an easy sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Stephen Sondheim musicals, dog breeds, Alan Partridge, and the Charles Paris novels by Simon Brett, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents as the losing semi-finalists battle it out for third place, with the two teams competing to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance. One of the sequence asks what links Heisenbug, Boggart, Weeping angel, and Schroedinger's cat

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first of the semi-finals sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hi-tech hotel in the self-governing Chinese gambling capital of Macau. Monica gets special access to a secret set of VIP suites dubbed The Mansion, where it takes more than just money to book an exclusive room. Giles visits a special theatre that sends its audience flying through the air, and helps to arrange the flowers for the Chinese New Year celebrations

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Black Mirror creator Charlie Brooker hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from December 2019, with broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Week in the Wild
programme.desc: Ex-politician Stanley Johnson, broadcaster and model Melanie Sykes, and comedian Henning Wehn tackle an iconic long-distance hike in Mexico's Copper Canyon, where they learn about the country's culture, people and environment

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team are in Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang prepare meals against the clock, using ingredients brought in by two friends who teach young people life skills. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Mandy and Chris adore their Victorian home in Manchester, but they cannot unlock the potential of their garden. Overlooked on all sides, the couple feel exposed whenever they spend time in it. Providing inspiration for the area are designers Helen Elks-Smith and Pip Probert. Meanwhile, Joel Bird meets Ben and Elaine, a Wiltshire couple who have an unusual outdoor space. Angela Scanlon hosts

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Gethin Jones presents study tips with the help of YouTuber Jack Edwards

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart examines Earth's unique atmosphere, revealing how it was initially made up of noxious volcanic gases until the emergence of life forms gave the planet oxygen. He also visits Argentina to explain how the atmosphere affects the weather - and almost loses his eyebrows during an experiment designed to demonstrate how methane is escaping through the permafrost in Siberia

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: David Harper takes on Philip Serrell at an antiques market in Maastricht in the Netherlands, where Philip struggles to find a bargain and David discovers a Victorian museum piece

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual melting of more than three million square miles of Arctic sea ice, which can be perilous for indigenous species. He also assesses fears that the region could be ice-free throughout the summer months in 50 years' time, and speculates on the results for native polar bears, whales and guillemot chicks

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham learns how foxes have been able to adapt and thrive in a world with an ever-increasing human population. New research reveals how the animals' split pupils enable them to hunt in the bright desert day, and the presenter discusses a theory that the creatures use the Earth's magnetic field to determine the location of their prey

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting St Albans Cathedral, Wolverhampton Art Gallery, and Packwood House in Warwickshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe in San Sebastian, where they tour the Spanish city's best tabernas sampling pintxos, before heading to Bordeaux

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a 2,000-mile journey along the famous American road, taking them through eight different states, each with their own history, flavours and people. They begin by learning about Chicago's meatpacking industry, before moving on to small-town American, where they spend time with the Amish community, cooking together and learning about their different way of life. Their first leg ends in St Louis, where they try 'concrete custard' - a treat so rich and thick it has to be served upside down

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Having survived seven tough design challenges, the two finalists go head-to-head to battle it out for the ultimate prize as they are challenged to transform luxury holiday villas in Cornwall, before Judge Michelle Ogundehin decides who will be this year's winner. Presented by Alan Carr

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: Police are called after a wealthy man suspected to be linked to drug crime was taken from his home in central Manchester by an armed gang. After locating the address where the victim was held and tortured, forensic evidence leads officers to name suspects, but while the hunt is on to find these dangerous criminals, another horrific kidnap happens

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tom Allen is the host for the first in a new run of stand-up showcases, performed in front of a live audience at London's world-famous Hammersmith Apollo. Each show features sets from two of the country's most acclaimed up-and-coming comedians, and this week it is Rosie Jones and Kae Kurd taking to the stage. Performers set to appear in this series include Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Guz Khan, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Rhys James, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The naturalist looks at deception, theft and cheating in the natural world, revealing which species are the leading fraudsters and how they fool others into getting what they want. Chris shows how sea otters kidnap pups and hold them to ransom for food, while white-winged choughs pressgang youngsters from another family into their own so they get extra help to feed their chicks

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at London's Shangri-La Hotel, Britain's highest hostelry. Their expectations of a standard business guest experience are blown away by discovering a hotel that makes guests' personal moments special. From bath butlers to marriage proposals, the place is a resort for celebrations in the clouds

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Former champions Irini Tzortzoglou and Jane Devonshire judge today's efforts, as five more contestants prepare signature meals and dishes involving their favourite ingredients. After one of the five cooks is eliminated from the contest, the remaining four must prepare a two-course meal to win one of the two remaining places in the quarter final

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to Somerset to work with Vicki and Bryan, who have a space-age geodome but have no idea how to transform it into a welcoming rental

Presenter Alan Carr and judge Michelle Ogundehin will then decide who makes it through to the next stage

programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Gethin Jones presents Wellbeing Wednesday, alongside science and French

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart examines the role of ice in changing the course of human evolution and shaping the planet's landscape, visiting a frozen waterfall, a unique lab in Arctic Norway and the granite peaks of Yosemite to learn more about the phenomenal power of glaciers. He also travels to Greenland to examine the evidence of climate change, and asks what the consequences will be if Earth's ice continues to melt

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: David Harper takes on Philip Serrell at a car boot sale in Chesterfield, where David finds an unusual antique record player, while Philip takes a trip to the Dean Forest Railway

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual return of half a billion Pacific salmon to the rivers of Canada and Alaska where they were born - and where bears hungrily await them. Every year, the fish travel up to 20,000 miles to spawn and die, swimming upstream and leaping over giant waterfalls as part of an extraordinary natural event that helps sustain the entire ecosystem

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores how the brain of the great ape has evolved, revealing that different areas have been adapted over time by the animal's anatomy, ingenuity and sociability. He also examines how the ability to use two hands asymmetrically sets the great apes apart from other tool-using animals, and how social living is linked to the evolution of the amygdala in both apes and humans

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charles Hanson and Adam Partridge unearth all manner of items to auction in Morecambe, Lancashire, including French porcelain and Japanese carvings. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the railway station that acted as the set for the iconic film Brief Encounter

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe, where they cook pizzas for locals in Bardi, Italy, before meeting one of their heroes in Rome

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit a biker bar in Missouri to find out about the origins of Route 66, before heading west, where they visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to the road. They also stop off for lunch in Kansas, sample Yorkshire pudding wraps in Tulsa and finally reach rural Oklahoma, where they cook with members of the Cherokee nation

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition returns for another series, four chefs are welcomed to the kitchen where they prepare canapes, starters and fish courses, inspired by this year's theme which celebrates British invention and innovation. With one of the four being sent home, the pressure is on to produce something delicious and ingenious

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: With the end of their provisions in sight, the officers contemplate a tough, risky strategy. To boost the men's morale, Fitzjames organises a carnival on the ice. Goodsir begins feeding Sir John's pet Capuchin monkey in order to prove that the canned food is causing lead poisoning

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Captain Crozier gives the order to abandon both ships, leaving a small group of men behind with enough provisions to last the winter, in the event a thaw frees the vessels. A series of flashbacks to 1845 tell the story of the voyage's beginnings through the eyes of an ordinary sailor

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton heads for Tiree, which boasts one of the best sunshine records in the UK

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Secret Science of Sewage
programme.desc: Dr George McGavin, Dr Zoe Laughlin and a team of world-leading scientists explore the biology, chemistry, natural history and social history of faeces from a TV-base camp like no other - within the pools, tanks and bowels of one of the UK's largest and most technologically advanced sewage works

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The presenter treks through the southern fjords of Norway to find the stunning Oseberg ship. This lost 1,000-year-old vessel transformed the fierce reputation of the Vikings and became a symbol of a nation's fight for freedom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants cook their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two participants, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. Gifted with their chosen favourite ingredient, as well as a larder, they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish that showcases it. Arbitrators in this edition include 2015 series champion Simon Wood and finalist Tony Rodd

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paints a wintry mountain scene using only grey and white tones

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate the first work of sculpture featured on the show, an unusual piece called The Gazing Head, which may have been made by Alberto Giacometti in 1930s Paris. The quest to uncover the truth is complicated by the fact that the sculpture was once broken into several pieces by a cat

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of a widowed 83-year-old who went undercover in a Chilean nursing home to investigate a private detective's concerns that the residents, including her father, were being abused. Despite an initial struggle with the technology involved, the aged spy adapted to his role, only to discover that the truth about the institution was very different to what his client had expected

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison presents the first of two programmes in which she follows the salt roads of North Africa, once the world's richest trading networks, to the fabled sandstone city of Timbuktu. Setting off from Tangier, she learns how the routes were forged by the demand for gold from the mines of sub-Saharan Africa. She then passes through the Islamic city Fes, home to the world's oldest university, catches a train to the great market town of Marrakech and treks across the Atlas Mountains, before visiting the ancient caves of salt, the commodity that gave the roads their name

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison embarks on a camel trek over the dunes of the Sahara, 3.5 million square miles of desert, for the second and final part of her journey to Timbuktu. Along the way she visits a livestock market that centuries ago provided merchants with their camels, before reaching her final destination. Far from the legendary City of Gold, Timbuktu is a city where UN solders keep a fragile peace after an incursion by Islamic extremists in 2012. Alice relives the city's glorious past with a visit to its world famous mosques, meets the Tuaregs - desert nomads who have sought refuge in there - and celebrates at a party with her newfound friends

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paints a wintry mountain scene using only grey and white tones

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lakeside view, surrounded by golden autumn trees

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Hacker's latest cost-cutting scheme has won the support of the prime minister - but Sir Humphrey is still determined to undermine it. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie exposes his insecurities and much more during a disastrous speech to the British Fruit Association. But it soon transpires that it's all part of his master plan. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Pauline Yates

programme.title: Donald Campbell: Speed King
programme.desc: Profile of the man who set eight world speed records on water and land in the 1950s and 1960s, using archive footage and first-hand testimonies from those who knew him. Campbell was spurred on by a desire to emulate his father Malcolm - who had also been a prolific record-breaker - but his career was dogged by ill fortune and bad weather. Jim Broadbent narrates

programme.title: Gravity and Me: The Force That Shapes Our Lives
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili investigates the amazing science of gravity, recreating groundbreaking experiments, including the moment when Galileo first worked out how to measure it. He investigates gravitational waves, finds out from astronauts what it's like to live without gravity, sets out to find where in Britain gravity is weakest and so where we weigh the least, and helps design a smartphone app that volunteers use to demonstrate how gravity affects time and makes us age at slightly different rates

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Palace Secrets
programme.desc: The historian goes behind closed doors at three treasure-filled royal palaces to uncover how each building has shaped a monarchy and a nation. With exclusive access to the palaces' most secret places, she uncovers the dramatic events that changed the course of British history, from might and intimidation at the Tower of London and the rise and fall of absolute monarchy at Hampton Court, to the modern face of royalty at Kensington Palace

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: With the rebellion reaching boiling point in Ireland, Elizabeth sends the dashing Earl of Essex to help quell the crisis. Glenda Jackson and Robin Ellis star

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: John Freeman quizzes Oscar-winning French actress Simone Signoret, famous for her roles in La Ronde, Les Diaboliques and Room at the Top

programme.title: France 1939: One Last Summer
programme.desc: A collection of home movies, diaries and letters from the last summer before the start of the Second World War reveal how far the impending conflict was away from the minds of ordinary French people. The film describes the final weeks of peace in France and the mix of blindness, denial and prophetic clear-sightedness of those facing the war that was about to unfold

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A chronicle of New York's drag scene in the 1980s, focusing on balls, voguing and the ambitions and dreams of those who gave the era its warmth and vitality

It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. programme.desc: Join seven-month-old Rosie and Fionn as they discover the world around them and see how their eyesight is improving. Fionn and his brother Odhrán are playing a game of peek-a-boo with mum and dad at home. Then the whole family head over to Loch Ness, where there are lots of exciting things to see. They even use some binoculars! Over in Ireland, Rosie’s brother Donnacha picks some colourful flowers at the garden centre to take home and show him. Back at home, Fionn is seeing more than his lunch, but what else can he see? In Rosie’s garden they plant their flowers and are inspired to finger-paint using all the colours from the rainbow, while Fionn’s family does a treasure hunt in the garden. They have to find their presents fast as someone on four legs is also hunting! Rosie’s family finish the day with a great game of hide-and-seek, searching high and low until everyone is found.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Febo's family are from America, where they celebrate Thanksgiving. As it is traditional on Thanksgiving to prepare and serve pumpkin pie as a dessert, Febo is making this for his friends. He starts by making a pastry case and fitting it into a pie dish. For the pumpkin filling: he pours melted butter and buttermilk into a bowl of brown sugar, along with whisked egg, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, allspice and cloves. Febo adds his special ingredient, pumpkin puree, and then mixes them all together. When it's mixed, he spoons it into the empty pastry case and puts it in the oven to cook. Febo's friends all help to decorate his American-themed table before they taste his pumpkin pie.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. It’s Safari Worlds annual recycling event, and Andy has been busy tidying up and encouraging the guests to recycle their litter. Mr Hammond calls as he is worried that the new clownfish are not settling in okay after their long trip from Borneo. They are leaving the safety of their sea anemone home, which they hardly ever do in the wild. programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Penny the Peacock Butterfly Caterpillar is on a mission. All her friends are busy eating nettles, but Penny longs for adventure. She wants to learn to fly, and she’s determined to succeed. She tries and tries again but can’t seem to manage it. It’s not until Auntie the Tawny Owl gives her some wise advice that something changes. Will Penny be able to fly now?

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour yellow, from cheese to chicks, and we explore yellow in spring time. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour yellow.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join eight-month-old Myles and Iffah as they discover the world around them and begin to gain some independence. Both children start the day with some breakfast. Myles’ mum is back to work for the first time since he was born, so he’s spending the day with dad. After breakfast, Iffah and her brother Walli play together - he does a drawing while she plays with her toy duck. Meanwhile, Myles and his dad heads to a local park, where they look at the lake and see lots of different birds. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. When Myles and his dad arrive at great grandma’s house, Myles reads a book with grandma and even turns the pages all on his own! After they join up with mum, they all visit a quarry to feed some fish. Iffah finishes the day with her family at a petting farm, where she gets to stroke an alpaca.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Aleisha's family comes from Uganda. Today, she is cooking the traditional meal of rolex, which is a Ugandan omelette. Aleisha uses tasty fresh vegetables such as baby tomatoes, cabbage, red onion and her special ingredient, mild green chilli, for her omelettes. She pours whisked eggs into baking tins and puts her vegetables on top. Then her recipe is ready to be cooked for her friends to enjoy. To top off the occasion, Aleisha wears a wonderful Ugandan outfit and has a musical surprise planned for her pals. Will Aleisha's friends guess her secret ingredient?

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. programme.desc: Join nine-month-old Bonnie and Harry as they discover the world around them and become more expressive. Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. Before leaving, there is much laughter as they decide to try and completely cover dad with sand. After their picnic, Harry’s decides to join his brother in having his photo taken, and it’s big smiles all around!

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Sebastiaan's making a South African bobotie, which is a local dish his family have shared with him. He starts by putting chopped spring onions, grated courgette, turmeric powder, ground coriander and torn red pepper into a casserole dish and cooking them. He then adds garlic puree, ginger puree, chutney and chopped tomatoes to cooked beef mince. Sebastiaan then carefully makes powder out of his special ingredient, cumin seeds, and adds the powder to the beef mixture. Sebastiaan mixes both the vegetables and the beef in an oven dish and flattens them before adding two bay leaves. He adds a topping of milk and egg and then places it in the oven. Sebastiaan has a wonderfully decorated table, and he has another traditional treat to go with his bobotie. Will his friends figure out what his special ingredient is?

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. programme.title: Lockdown Live: What Next?
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Nick Robinson present this live BBC News special, one year on since the UK's first lockdown. Naga and Nick reflect on some of the biggest lockdown stories of the year, and look forward to ask how the world is going to change beyond the pandemic

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The news of Katy's arrest becomes common knowledge and Mick fears that everyone knows it is about him. Meanwhile, Frankie meets Stuart at the car lot office. Ruby makes her feelings about the Slaters pretty clear to Kush, while Jean comes up with a plan to save Stacey. Racist graffiti on the Minute Mart brings up painful memories for the Panesars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Two members of staff find themselves in a tight spot, as Louis is bleeding out after being attacked by an old friend, while Cameron still has Ange held hostage - although the truth of his crimes is coming to light. Can he lie his way out of Holby or will those he has tricked manage to save their colleague end his reign of terror? Meanwhile, Dom returns to work after his operation and his decision to sue the hospital - and despite his attempts to forget the past, a petty patient soon revives his hunger for revenge

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the last of the heats which sees five more hopefuls competing for an apron by cooking their signature dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. The two best will automatically go through, while the remaining three must cook two courses for John and Gregg as well as Dhruv Baker and Alex Rushmer - champion and finalist from the 2010 competition

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary explores why it has taken so long for the full scale of child abuse in football to emerge. The documentary follows the investigation into former Southampton youth coach Bob Higgins, filming alongside Hampshire Police detectives as they conduct interviews with Higgins and the survivors

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Sheriff removes Zach Shelby's body from Norma's room. She is certain that it is Jake Abernathy's doing, and when flowers arrive anonymously she interprets the gesture as a veiled threat. The sadistic mother decides that buying the motel was a mistake and makes plans to move out, but her son makes clear his objections

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: With Jake Abernathy expecting her to meet him at midnight with the $150,000, Norma goes to Sheriff Romero, who assures her that he will take care of the situation and that she has nothing to worry about. Not quite assured, she asks Dylan to get her a gun and show her how to use it. Meanwhile, Romero visits Keith Summers' sister Maggie and learns Jake's real name

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Peak District with a couple returning from the Netherlands, who want their generous £1.5million budget to provide accommodation for their visiting mothers

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband and wife Raymond and Karen are both vegan but are bored of making the same old thing. Paired with chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, they hope to come away with some new dishes for their repertoire. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Furniture restorer Will Kirk is presented with a damaged mother of pearl table, badly burned after a hot pan was mistakenly placed on its surface. And mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a broken toy aeroplane that doubles up as a memento of a lost childhood in Iraq

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating mark the start of the first lockdown with an hour-long special looking at some of the more positive stories and achievements of the past 12 months, and celebrating how people across the country have stepped up to help others

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Belgium v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents action from both teams' opening 2022 FIFA World Cup Group E qualifier at Den Dreef in Leuven, as Wales took on the team at the top of the world rankings

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: The Surrogates
programme.desc: Caitlin is now pregnant with her boss Kate's baby, and they navigate the complex relationship between a surrogate and intended parent, while also working together every day. programme.desc: Paul Murton's final loch-hopping journey begins in the forgotten lands of Morvern. At Fort William he paces the ruins of the original fort and learns how to make Jacobite bank notes, before ending his journey at a 'loch that never was' beneath the 300ft Steall Falls
