programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jeanette and Ian have dreamed for years about owning a holiday home by Spain's popular Mar Menor, and with retirement approaching they feel it's the right time to make that dream come true. Scarlette Douglas impresses them with five properties to choose between within their budget of £75,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge design an eco-friendly bedroom for a teenager, creating the bed, shelving and picture frames from pallets, and a swing chair using the art of macramé. Dick rolls up his sleeves to show Chris how to re-lay and re-point a patio to help make his garden a safe sanctuary for his elderly mum, while Angel teams up with her fellow vintage fan Rachel to breathe life back into an old drinks trolley, using the 17th-century French art of decoupage. Amid it all, life at the chateau goes on, as Dick continues his project of converting an old stable into his dream potting shed

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cambridgeshire, where first-time self-builders Mark and Helen Farmer are trying to construct a traditional home appropriate for modern living. Charlie tries to keep them focused on the details, but it's the big stuff that threatens to trip them up. When Mark and Helen break ground, they discover Roman and Anglo-Saxon artefacts on site, a surprise that threatens to bring the project to a standstill

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. King's Head landlady Helen challenges Louise and Andy about the problems they reported on cleanliness in the bathroom. while Louise and Andy from Beck Hall get hot under the collar when the others criticise their dog-friendly approach

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team heads to Sue's house in Tiverton, Devon, finding a moped that brings back memories, and fighting over a petrol gauge that Simon O'Brien wants to upcycle into a clock

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers notice the Asian short-clawed otters begin to develop worrying bald patches and call in the vet. The flamingo chicks are being moved from the creche to the adult aviary, but two birds who have bonded face being split up as one of them is limping and has to stay behind. A pregnant antelope's aggressive behaviour is causing concern

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Xuxa and Simon, who are keen to transform the Second World War air raid shelter in their garden into a craft room - the only problem is, the space is tiny, so Angel thinks up some creative space-saving solutions. They also get requests to help Lyra and her dad James, who want to make a new chicken coop, and Helena, whose 1960s stereogram record player is no longer spinning

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Ian and Sophie Cooper, who aim to restore a 200-year-old industrial building in Somerset. The couple plan to build a large glass extension to make the most of the incredible views on offer, but run into trouble when they learn the building has no foundations

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth explore the Staffordshire and Worcestershire Canal. They uncover the history of Stourport, which only came into being after the canal was built there, which is unique for a British town. They explore the history of carpet manufacturing in Kidderminster and meet 84-year-old Audrey, who operated a daunting power loom for much of her working life. Gyles also dons a panto costume in Stourbridge to learn more about the local legend of Dick Whittington. When the duo reach Wolverhampton, they get a taste of the Desi pub, a modern institution in the Black Country that combines Punjabi food with British booze

programme.title: Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels from Cardigan Bay to Liverpool, visiting the heritage railway in Tywyn that was the inspiration for Thomas the Tank Engine and a community that is famous for knitting. She also paints a carriage in Boston Lodge, catches the famous Ffestiniog railway through Snowdonia, and visits Europe's fastest zip wire and largest pet cemetery

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The emergency room documentary continues, highlighting the stories of people facing adversity head-on. Erin, a 61-year-old former cancer patient with an ear infection, is rushed to St George's Hospital after she collapsed at home. Doctors fear the condition may have spread to her brain, and send her for a CT scan to find out more. Tony, a 70-year-old part-time DJ, arrives in A&E after being involved in a high-speed bicycle accident while training for a 100-mile charity bike ride, and Charlie, a 39-year-old cabin crew worker, turns up at the hospital complaining of unbearable pain after discharging himself during a previous visit, and removing the cast fitted to support his broken arm

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz. Guest captain Richard Ayoade is joined by Rob Beckett, going up against Jon Richardson and Claudia Winkleman, with Adam Riches accompanying Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley dishing out the all-important consonants, vowels and numbers

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth explore the Staffordshire and Worcestershire Canal. They uncover the history of Stourport, which only came into being after the canal was built there, which is unique for a British town. They explore the history of carpet manufacturing in Kidderminster and meet 84-year-old Audrey, who operated a daunting power loom for much of her working life. Gyles also dons a panto costume in Stourbridge to learn more about the local legend of Dick Whittington. When the duo reach Wolverhampton, they get a taste of the Desi pub, a modern institution in the Black Country that combines Punjabi food with British booze

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The emergency room documentary continues, highlighting the stories of people facing adversity head-on. Erin, a 61-year-old former cancer patient with an ear infection, is rushed to St George's Hospital after she collapsed at home. Doctors fear the condition may have spread to her brain, and send her for a CT scan to find out more. Tony, a 70-year-old part-time DJ, arrives in A&E after being involved in a high-speed bicycle accident while training for a 100-mile charity bike ride, and Charlie, a 39-year-old cabin crew worker, turns up at the hospital complaining of unbearable pain after discharging himself during a previous visit, and removing the cast fitted to support his broken arm

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Germany to learn the secret behind potato crisp, while Helen Lawal investigates whether carb-restrictive diets really burn fat quicker, and uncovers an eye-opening medical use for this popular weight-loss tool. Plus, Matt Tebbutt finds out the surprising reason why smoked fish is dyed yellow

programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Master sculptor and gilder Adam Williamson teaches Kirstie Allsop how to apply gold leaf to a second-hand picture frame to give it a luxury look and feel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows a range of properties in the south of Spain's Costa Blanca to Jacqui, who is on a mission to find her dream holiday home to share with her husband Adrian

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Xuxa and Simon, who are keen to transform the Second World War air raid shelter in their garden into a craft room - the only problem is, the space is tiny, so Angel thinks up some creative space-saving solutions. They also get requests to help Lyra and her dad James, who want to make a new chicken coop, and Helena, whose 1960s stereogram record player is no longer spinning

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Tamasin and Andy Jarrett, who hope to build a modern family home in the middle of a Kent conservation area, but they prove reluctant to take on any new ideas

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners visit Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk, run by husband-and-wife duo Clive and Jenny Aylett, but when the pair's rivals arrive, Pauline Cox and Claire Smith have difficulty locating their favourite appliance. Publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne are astonished to discover they cannot lock their room's door. In the morning, some of the guest make awkward requests to change their breakfast orders, causing professional chef Clive to experience a meltdown in the kitchen that leads to a fundamental error

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset for the third day of the competition, where they are wowed by Karen and Mike Hepker's luxury castle experience. Although the guests are disappointed at the bathroom fixtures and fittings, they are given the chance to vent their emotions during an afternoon of painting in the castle grounds. Plus, tensions rise between Mike and Tash at dinner, and harsh comments at feedback lead the hosts to suspect game playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, run by publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne. Karen Hepker is disappointed with the honeymoon suite, while Clive and Jenny Aylett find a dubious looking splash guard in the shower. A boat tour backfires when Jenny struggles with her water phobia and at dinner Karen takes the opportunity to tell the others exactly what she thinks of them. But things really kick off when it comes to feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wharfe House hosts Pauline Cox and Claire Smith begin a bitter debate, demanding to know why their beds caused such a sleepless night. Accusations subsequently fly in all directions and walk-outs look on the cards as tensions mount leading to the payments being revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find an antique cannon in a north Wales dealer's storage shed, as well as giving restorer Gemma a crash course in croquet

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Dagmar the giraffe is supporting her closest herd member Orla, whose new calf is overdue - but at the same time she is reluctant to allow her own youngest to feed properly. So the keepers plan a herd birth in the hope that both mothers can learn from each other. There is concern when two of the spider monkeys withdraw from their family, while two devoted camels experience the trauma of separation when one undergoes an operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Natalie and Pete come up with some compact camping ideas, such as a portable solar shower that doubles up as a sink and tyre chairs with a rustic rope finish. Dick also helps a viewer transform her garden into a plot she can be proud of, and Angel offers upholstery advice to a woman whose favourite chair has been left in tatters by the family dog

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Londoner Chris Ostwald decides to build a New England-style water mill in the Chilterns, on a hill and nowhere near any water, after being inspired by an illustration on a yoghurt pot. He bought the plot 20 years previously and it has always been his dream to build a retreat for himself and his family. Everything seems to be going smoothly, until a neighbour complains the house is breaking its planning consent. Kevin McCloud presents

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The look back at the epic transformation of Dick Strawbridge and wife Angel's 45-room chateau continues, focusing on how Angel's creativity brought the chateau back to life, as walls were smashed, exquisitely designed rooms created and stunning bathroom suites installed

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Mental health nurse Jim counsels people who are struggling with self-isolation, while paramedics Karina and James respond to a call from a man who claims to be armed and is threatening suicide. Despite losing his mother to Covid-19, paramedic Richard continues working on the frontline while also managing his anxiety about taking the virus home to his wife, whose complex health issues put her at high risk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary detailing the daily work of staff at St George's Hospital in south-west London continues, this time highlighting the stories of patients who received support from loved ones when life became tough. Gerald, a 66-year-old trained lifeguard and polo club official is rushed into A&E, having sustained a serious head wound after a 10-foot fall. The friends and family of 55-year-old Beverly gather at the hospital when she is admitted with a broken leg after being hit by a car near her home, and Paul, a 39-year-old father-of-three, arrives in resus with a nasty cut to his right arm. Paul complains that he is unable to move his fingers, leading staff to believe be may have significant tendon or nerve damage that could leave him permanently unable to use his hand

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words and numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Danny Dyer taking on Joe Wilkinson and Gabby Logan, while David O'Doherty joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Mental health nurse Jim counsels people who are struggling with self-isolation, while paramedics Karina and James respond to a call from a man who claims to be armed and is threatening suicide. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of the team's favourite investigations. Kate Quilton visits Haagen-Dazs to find out why some vanilla ice-creams are more expensive than others, while Helen Lawal heads to Tanzania to see the lengths vanilla farmers go to in the production of the natural flavouring. Matt Tebbutt travels to the Netherlands to explore the myth that Edam was once used as emergency cannon balls and Jimmy Doherty learns about an innovative solution to the problem of shellfish feeding on discarded plastic in the sea

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dancehall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at North Bridge Street B&B in Hawick in the Scottish Borders, which is run by Eileen Walsh and her Portuguese partner Emanuel Forte. When the guests arrive, the purple-themed rooms cause a reaction, while one competitor's constant criticising irritates another

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at the Golden Fleece in Ruleholme, Cumbria, run by chef Robert Cowan and his partner, Sylvia Spark. Before long, guests Eileen and Emmanuel pick up on cleanliness issues, and breakfast proves to be a disappointment

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Bubble Inn in Stenson, Derbyshire for the third day of the competition, where unsightly stains and security leave some of them less than impressed. Manager and ex-semi-professional boxer Connie Eleftheriou is keen to take his rivals in the ring with him, however the real damage happens when Emmanuel is taken to hospital after some overenthusiastic dancing at a Greek-themed night

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Melinda Beckett-Hughes is the final host of the week at Ayuda House in Greater Manchester - and as she has spent the week picking fault, the guests expect perfection from her. However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some old scores. Eileen and Emmanuel from North Bridge Street want to get to the bottom of Melinda and Stewart's dirty bin comments as well as challenging Melinda's shower issues. Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien come across a gramophone and a stow-away bed to restore. Meanwhile, Simon accidently puts his foot through a drum

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Keepers introduce a new male to the spotty chocolate poison dart frog colony, hoping that the competition will result in an increase in tadpoles. Meanwhile, the Sulawesi macaques are ecstatic to welcome a new baby to their family, but their enthusiasm is a cause for concern as new mother Maggie may not be able to protect her baby from the other females

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: It's springtime at the chateau, and with the wedding season just a few weeks away, completing phase two of the basement project is becoming an urgent priority for Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree. To cater for all their summer guests, a much-needed utility and laundry room is about to be plumbed in. Angel comes up with a unique way of preserving the 150-year-old wallpaper, while Dick wrestles with a rake while working on the meadow

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Sarah and Dean Berry, who returned to live in their home town of Newport, Gwent, after 17 years in London. The couple have bought an 18th-century folly - a castle perched on a hilltop - and plan to restore the building to its former glory, while also wrapping a large modern extension around the original structure

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Back in 2017, George drafted in architect Chris Mason to help Wendy and Allan, whose Colchester home resembled a block of flats. They ended up with an enormous double-height extension and a Roman-style portico, but the ambitious scheme blew their budget and the project remained unfinished. George returns to find out whether they have managed to finish the property to the same exceptional standards

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey through north-west England, Michael Portillo makes a clean sweep in Ashley, Cheshire, where in Victorian times the new middle classes set up home in suburban villas with multiple chimneys swept by children. In Macclesfield, he finds the end of the Silk Route and tries his hand at screen printing. Then after stoking the fire on the steam-powered Churnet Valley Railway, the former politician alights at Froghall, Staffordshire, heading for Alton Towers to trace the 19th-century origins of the theme park

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new railway journey along the south coast of England. Beginning in the port of Dover, he takes a plunge into the English Channel, inspired by the example set by a brave 19th-century sea captain. A miniature steam train one third the size of a conventional locomotive conveys him from Romney Marsh to Dungeness, and in Eastbourne, he learns how the 7th Duke of Devonshire managed to market the town's attractions to the refined upper-crust of Victorian London. The first leg of his trip concludes in eccentric style at the Glyndebourne opera festival on the South Downs

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Newhaven to Worthing, examining the nation's hidden defences, and taking in two magnificent engineering achievements - the Ouse Valley Viaduct and the Clayton Tunnel. programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built

programme.desc: Business manager Alison Bennett hosts the first dinner party in Glasgow, where she hopes that her Thai menu of chicken satay, red-roasted pork and chili chocolate combs will help to secure her the £1,000 prize. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Butcher Graeme Curley hosts the second night in Glasgow, where he has planned a meaty menu complete with pork ribs. He hopes to wow his guests by serving steak - and by bringing some of his trade to the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Michelle Blackett is the host on day three in Glasgow, and hopes to impress the diners by preparing a menu of exceedingly well-appointed food. Over the course of the evening, Michelle's guests discover her hidden heritage, and hospitality student Erwin - who speaks five languages - does his best to add the 'Glaswegian' dialect to his arsenal

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ballet enthusiast and hospitality management student Erwin Orduz hosts the fourth dinner party in Glasgow and hopes to impress his guests with an evening of shocks and surprises. He attempts hotel-style presentation and serves up his meals in a dining room that has been decorated with a menagerie theme of animals and nakedness

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Yvonne Finlayson hosts the final night of the cooking competition in Glasgow, offering her guests a combination of 'scientifically-infused' food, high-octane entertainment and scintillating chat - plus a healthy dose of revelations - to try to claim the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The owners of Jack's Waterfront at St Clair Shores in Michigan are keen to hear the fiery chef's advice for turning their failing seafood restaurant around. The lakeside location is a popular summer spot with fantastic views, but an uninspiring menu and a manager more skilled at socialising than organising are preventing them from achieving year-round success

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps hairdresser Kim and car salesman Steve find their first property together. Kim and Steve have been together for six years and have holidayed several times on the Spanish island of Gran Canaria. With a budget of £120,000, they can't wait to turn their dream of a romantic bolthole into a reality

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bettina and Eamon have spent nearly 30 years in military accommodation moving with Eamon's job. They are now ready to start the next chapter of their lives by buying the first property they will have bought together. They have chosen the Costa Calida in Spain and are hoping one of the five properties Danni Menzies has found within their £100,000 budget prove be perfect for them

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the railways crossing the Scottish Highlands, and meeting both the workers who keep them running and the steam enthusiasts who safeguard these iconic routes. The programme takes the Strathspey steam railway through the Cairngorms National Park, taking in a charity abseiling event at the Forth Bridge along the way

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Cheeky Jack Russell Rocco seeks a new home after his loving owner's health took a turn for the worse, setting his sights on Charnelle and Holly and their seven-year-old son Reay. However, they have their minds set on a French bulldog. Later, fur flies as Karen and Stefano meet two of Wood Green's biggest and hairiest residents - husky brother and sister Olli and Kalli. Jess is looking for unconditional love after a recent break-up and Staffordshire bull terrier Gloria might well be the ideal buddy she needs right now

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With autumn just around the corner, it's a chance for the Strawbridge family to make the most of the wild larder on their doorstep. Dick is determined to make sloe gin, while Angel presses on with her plan to create a library in the pepper pot tower

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows. This week they consider The Circle, CBeebies Bedtime Story, Escape from Pretoria, Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites, Baywatch, This Morning, Naked Attraction, and BBC news coverage of Prince Philip's recovery

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: A 23-year-old man with a history of violence is questioned over a string of offences that have left victims badly hurt, but refuses to give any answers. Without fresh evidence, the police has no choice but to release the suspect. Within days of leaving police custody, he reoffends and officially ends up on the Most Wanted list

programme.title: Caroline Flack: Her Life and Death
programme.desc: Documentary tribute to the TV presenter, who took her own life in February 2020, delving beyond the headlines to reveal the complex woman behind the public persona. The film charts Caroline's career, from stage school to some of the biggest shows on TV, and hears from family and colleagues - including Dermot O'Leary and Olly Murs - about the pressures that fame, mental health issues, press and social media had on Caroline

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas presents as four more famous faces put their baking skills to the test for charity. Hollywood star James McAvoy, singer and Voice coach Anne-Marie, writer and comedian David Baddiel and former athlete Kelly Holmes are this week's quartet, whose challenges involve decorative tarts, a savoury technical and rendering animals in cake form

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows highlighting the way working from home is changing the way many people live. Northampton newlyweds Nikki and Andy both worked from home but their cluttered terrace was close to bursting point. Meanwhile, Amber ran her own fitness classes from her home and wanted somewhere brighter and bigger for her clients. Could Kirstie persuade them to go with her redesigns or would Phil find either couple a much more suitable new place?

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Inspired by a 600-year old Welsh legend, George Clarke recruits Will Hardie and a team of skilled builders. Together they design a lakeside cabin built using the natural raw materials to be found in the surrounding lakes, hills and valleys near Machynlleth in Wales. They then have just two days to complete all preparations for this epic challenge before constructing the project in a day

programme.title: Drawers Off

Plus, the a husband-and-wife team in Majorca who invested big money after losing one of their business partners and a catch-up with a fruit juice entrepreneur on the French Riviera, who has tried to expand into new markets.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Richard has dreamed of owning a tropical holiday home for more than 20 years, and thinks Nosara in Costa Rica is the perfect place. He's brought best his friend Emma along for support, and with a budget of £275,000 and five options lined up by property expert Danni Menzies, hopes to find his dream place

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton offer their help to Margaret Wagstaff and Keith Turner, who want to spend their retirement somewhere other than Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, but are unable to decide whether they will get more for their money in Cornwall or Collioure, south-west France. However, a bigger problem emerges when former classmates John Mabey and Martin Young renew old hostilities, so Scott tries to lighten the mood by taking everyone for a dance in 'Essex's smallest disco' - his kitchen

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Laura Hamilton and Jonnie Irwin help meet the couple's property desires

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Marketing manager Clair Sherwood-Parkin hosts the first party in Darlington, Co Durham, hoping to impress with a Moroccan-inspired feast. However, one guest causes a stir when she introduces herself as the Queen of Sheba, and Clair's party game unearths some shocking truths about her diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon James Emson hosts the second party in Darlington, Co Durham, and decides to accompany his curry main course with a gruesome contest in which he serves up a selection of eyeballs, tongues and other bits of offal, then challenges his guests to identify which animal they came from. Diner Barry l'Anson also manages to leave the party having achieved a lifetime ambition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barry I'Anson hosts the third dinner party in Darlington, Co Durham, and hopes his love-themed evening will endear him to his guests. Unfortunately for him Clair Sherwood-Parkin is not impressed and begins doubting the host's masculinity, and vet James Emson is concerned what the dessert will be like. Charlotte Langley finds herself wanting a sick bag before the evening is over, and Eseyoma Sodje steals the show during some cringe-worthy entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charlotte Langley hosts the fourth dinner party in Darlington, Co Durham, and asks her guests to attend dressed as their favourite red-carpet celebrity, but as the night progresses, she discovers she is not very good at taking criticism and a tense situation threatens to get out of hand

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eseyoma Sodje hosts the final dinner party in Darlington, Co Durham, hoping her Nigerian recipes and a hint of glamour will impress her guests. To further improve her chances of bagging the £1,000 prize, she puts on a round of hip-hop karaoke, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Angela and Steve from Leicestershire search for a second home in Lanzarote that they can move to permanently after retirement, with a budget of £225,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Lynne from Yorkshire have devoted their lives to looking after their six children and foster children. Now they're ready to find a holiday home in southern Spain that they can use for extended holidays with all their family. Laura Hamilton pulls out all the stops to show them five properties around Mojácar for their budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows Sri Lankan couple Kulan and Kamala five properties for their budget of £200,000 in Portugal's Algarve region. However, it remains to be seen which property has the most 'wow' factor to become their dream holiday home

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: MC Dizzee Rascal, radio DJ Nick Grimshaw, psychologist Philippa Perry and actor-writer Reece Shearsmith brave the baking tent in the name of charity. All that stands between them and the title of Star Baker are three challenges - vegetable slices, a 'right royal' technical and a biscuit selfie depicting themselves at work. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith judge their efforts, while Matt Lucas shouts out how much time is remaining in a silly voice

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred and his team of matchmakers invite more singletons to the luxury hotel in the hope of finding love in the sun. A string of unhappy relationships has left 24-year-old Thalia with a phobia of kissing. Her date with 25-year-old Essex lad Jack date gets off to a great start after a discovery of a shared love of sharks. Widower Tony is looking for someone to share his twilight years and is paired with the glamorous Lesley, a fellow East Ender. However, a surprising differing of political opinions threatens to derail their date

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows that highlighted the need to get the balance between home and garden just right. In Bury St Edmunds, Lauren Grant believes the opportunity to build on a land she and husband Christian bought from the council will transform their confused downstairs. He, however, just wants rid of the house altogether. Meanwhile, Harrogate couple Carol and Andrew need extra space for Grandad. Can Kirstie build into their sizeable garden or will Phil find somewhere else more suitable?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a home built on an airfield in Strathaven in Scotland to check on the progress made by Colin MacKinnon and his partner Marta Briongos. Their ambitious plan was to build a metal-sculptural home inspired by aircraft hangars right next to the runway, but the difficulties of building their elegant design saw a year-long project stretch to three years

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes

programme.desc: Letting agent Paul is called to a houseshare after fighting, cannabis use and partying drive one resident out of the property establishes the source of the problem is a young couple. Harrow housing agents Andrew and Jo find a suspected unlicensed multiple-occupancy house in gross disrepair. After tracking down the landlord, they have mercy and allow him to spend his £5,000 fine on fixing up the property, in the hope he will turn it into a nice family home. But will the he be as good as his word?

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The housing team in Harrow accompany the police on a raid after receiving reports of dangerous conditions at a home belonging to a notorious slumlord's brother. Lettings agent John wants to turn a former Blackpool hotel into bedsits, but a routine check on the property reveals not only a severely neglected house, but also a drugs den littered with paraphernalia and some of the worst conditions he has ever witnessed

Elsewhere, Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner face evicting people from three properties owned by the same landlord in north London, and Brian and Delroy Anglin collect £2,000 debt owed to a vet in Kent

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim is on the trail of a speeding motorcyclist, Jon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan and dog handler Barry is brought to book by a would-be lawyer. Meanwhile, Sarah deals with the aftermath of a drunken brawl between two brothers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sgt Baxter has his work cut out when confronted by a dishonest driver. programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers are forced to exercise caution when they stop an uninsured driver who is known to have a violent past, while Jon Peacock and police dog Brad are on the hunt for suspected burglars. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner are in Birmingham on a potentially dangerous, full-scale eviction of a large traveller community. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.desc: A girl is killed in a crime displaying all the hallmarks of white supremacists who recruit teenagers to carry out acts of violence, and the team races to bring the ringleader to justice before more youngsters are butchered. Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives respond to a hold-up committed by a bloodthirsty team of robbers in ski-masks, and McCoy struggles to determine whether the teenage tearaway found with the criminals was an unwilling hostage or a active participant in the crimes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An NCIS polygraph specialist comes under suspicion when a murder occurs while he is moonlighting, and the agents step in to determine whether their colleague is responsible. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A navy lieutenant's car careers off the road and he is found to have been murdered behind the wheel. NCIS is called in to investigate and Tony becomes obsessed with finding the victim's sister, a well-known TV reporter whose disappearance is seemingly linked to the case - and several others. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a mutilated body is found in a car at a scrapyard, the NCIS agents find themselves trying to locate a serial killer. Meanwhile, Abby and McGee head to Mexico and investigate the 20-year-old mystery of a drug dealer's murder. Their actions attract the attention of the head of a local drug cartel, and links begin to emerge between their case, the serial killings and Gibbs's past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When the lead suspect of an unsolved and high-profile robbery case is released from prison, the NCIS team members resume their investigation with an undercover stakeout

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A bungled diamond robbery leaves three innocent bystanders dead, prompting the team to embark on a desperate hunt for the killers - but another slaying is soon discovered, apparently perpetrated by the Mob. Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan star

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the deaths of a museum curator and an art authenticator. Initial evidence points to a murder and suicide, but a fake Monet masterpiece discovered in the studio helps expose a lucrative racket involving priceless forged paintings believed to have been sunk in a Nazi U-boat in 1945. Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe star

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Lindsay receives a combat cross medal for bravery, and new recruit Jo Danville stumbles over a body in the crime lab on her first day in the role. The detectives discover the victim worked in one of the building's other offices, and Adam finds evidence suggesting death was premeditated. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Adam witnesses a murder on a video chat website, and the team races to the victim's apartment, where a search reveals a significant clue - her laptop has gone missing. Inquiries eventually lead to a college professor who implicates the dead girl's boyfriend - and as the case unfolds, it emerges that the killer is lining up a second victim. Crime drama, starring AJ Buckley, Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: First episode of the fantasy crime drama about a detective with the ability to see supernatural creatures, one of whom is behind a kidnapping case. Starring David Giuntoli

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank investigate the case of a teenager who was attacked while breaking into a house, and finds the homeowners are not what they appear to be

programme.title: Bull

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murders of two scientists found stabbed to death following a fire. The evidence points to a mentally disturbed woman, but Cutter and Rubirosa suspect she may have been manipulated by someone else

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The chief suspect in the murder of several homeless men appears to be a schizophrenic street performer. Detectives Lupo and Bernard's problems compound when they find evidence suggesting a possible copycat is at work. Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A gay city councillor is shot dead after a meeting, and the trail of suspicion leads the team to a bigoted rival politician and a male prostitute. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis re-examine a case closed since the 1960s after a vehicle containing the remains of a murdered man is dredged up from the Hudson River. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis examine the murder of a popular Harlem community leader, while McCoy and Carmichael struggle to prevent a chaotic situation. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston, Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis try to solve the murder of a college student whose death was depicted in graphic detail on the internet. However, the investigation grinds to a halt when they encounter a lawyer who is unwilling to reveal vital information about her client's past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. She tries to persuade him to aid her master plan by threatening to kill his father. Alejandro Rivera arrives with a platoon of Mexican troops, but hopes of rescue may be premature. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames uncover how a rich woman's lust for fame and a professional fundraiser's desperation to pay off a loan shark may have led to the murder of two people. Police drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a pregnant woman with a drug habit. Goren and Eames focus their search on the victim's boyfriend and are highly suspicious of his story, but further evidence leads them to realise other members of his wealthy family may have been involved. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the death of church sexton Morris Abernathy and discover the victim had a relationship with the killer, leading to a widening scandal involving money embezzled from the collection funds. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A couple are attacked in their home, and while the male victim is found dead at the scene, Mac is able to question the severely injured wife, who identifies her own son as the assailant. However, a convicted drug dealer confesses to the crime, forcing the team to investigate his motives. Guest starring Marcos De la Cruz (Sons of Anarchy)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The head of a notorious Puerto Rican gang is found dead outside a hotel shortly after his predecessor is released from jail. Mac is forced to use his diplomatic skills to prevent a revenge attack - but a second death and an ambush on a police car lead him to believe the killer is a disgruntled insider. Drama, starring Gary Sinise, with Edward James Olmos (Battlestar Galactica, Blade Runner)

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank investigate the case of a female lawyer found dead on a bus, having been forcibly injected with a fatal overdose of bee venom causing an anaphylactic shock. Monroe helps determine it to be the work of a feral bee queen, the sworn enemy of Hexebiests and the investigators' attention switches to a legal case the victim was working on just prior to her death

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: A woman is struck by a car while suffering terrifying hallucinations. Though seriously injured she survives the accident, but during the commotion an unidentified man arrives on the scene and strangles her. Nick and Hank investigate, and discover a goat-like creature that could be behind a series of recent disappearances

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Paramedics become suspicious about a baby's death, calling in Briscoe and Curtis, who discover the parents adopted the child from an illegal Russian ring. The case takes on another dimension altogether when it transpires that the crooks specialise in placing terminally ill infants with unsuspecting families. Starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Carmichael have the unenviable task of prosecuting three police officers with a racially motivated hate crime that threatens to cause uproar in the city. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Angie Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Tensions are high between new partners Briscoe and Curtis as they investigate the murder of a wealthy student in a biker bar. The detectives discover the victim's father had offered him $10,000 to drop his hobby and his biker girlfriend. Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A wealthy Wall Street stockbroker's death looks like suicide, but Logan suspects otherwise and pins the blame on the man's protege. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis join forces with their Baltimore colleagues to investigate the murder of a government official. It soon emerges that the deceased had been stalked by a man because of her lesbian affair with a married woman, who pleads for her identity to be kept secret. However, an over-zealous attorney embarks on a crusade to make the information public knowledge, going so far as to take the officers to court in an effort to force them to reveal their source - but his interest in the case raises suspicions

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a prostitute leaves an eminent plastic surgeon as the most obvious culprit. However, Kincaid suspects otherwise, and sets out to discover whether the man was framed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. An indentation on the victim's skull leads the agents to believe he was killed on a British ship, but collecting evidence from the vessel proves challenging. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: L.A.'s Finest
programme.desc: McKenna agrees to help Syd get the stolen fentanyl into police custody without anyone guessing their involvement. Izzy's strange behaviour distracts McKenna

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate when it is revealed that a series of female murder victims are all ex-girlfriends of a notoriously abusive lawyer. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A conman is found beaten to death in a hotel room, prompting Goren and Eames to look for a group of investors he swindled. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.desc: Armed robbers break into the apartment of lawyer Roland Carson and make off in a helicopter with jewels worth more than $5million. The detectives investigate and, as people who have come into contact with the gems start to turn up dead, they suspect Carson could have staged the whole affair and killed his accomplices to cover his tracks. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A couple whose student son has been missing for a week fear the worst when they discover a badly decomposed body on the roof of a downtown warehouse, and the CSIs are called to investigate further. However, the case is turned on its head when the autopsy reveals the dead man's true identity, and an anonymous phone call leads the team on a wild chase through the city. Crime drama, guest starring Helen Slater

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank investigate the death of a popular music teacher eaten by rats in his car. A feral-like character running a pest control firm seems a likely suspect. However, when the parent is arrested and abused in jail, the boy snaps and sets a trap for the rich children who routinely abuse and bully him

programme.title: Grimm

Denise, who was diagnosed with obsessive compulsive disorder in 2002, is taking four days out of her own cleaning regimen to make life easier for 82-year-old former British Aerospace engineer Frank from Chipping Sodbury, south Gloucestershire, while hairdresser Adam heads to Buckinghamshire to help Helen with her piles of clutter. The cleaning agency also takes on the challenge of sprucing up an underpass in Redhill, Surrey

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.desc: An Irish music hall impresario comes to London to seek his fortune, and makes it big by buying a rundown theatre and transforming it into a major attraction. Drama based on the life of impresario George Edwardes, starring Richard Greene, Ann Todd, Hazel Court, Charles Victor and Peter Graves

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. programme.title: All You Need Is Love

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: The tree gang offer their specialist attention to the 200-year-old Almeida Botanic Gardens in Gibraltar before getting into gear for the spring. Back at Kew, Tony Hall gives students advice while replanting islands in one of Kew's lakes, and excitement mounts as the oldest pot plant in the world shows signs of producing a cone - for the first time in 180 years. Not to be outshone by this remarkable feat, Jon Hammerton becomes the father of a baby girl

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Anne tells Mike she thinks they ought to spice up their sex life by experimenting with a spot of bondage _ but isn't prepared for the disconcerting enthusiasm with which he embraces the idea. It isn't just his Heath Robinson system of ropes and pulleys she objects to, but the discovery that he's started sharing all their intimate secrets with the lads back at the shop

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Steve accidentally slices off his finger while using a circular saw, causing all-out panic in the shop, while Mike's girlfriend Anne does the unthinkable and goes shopping at a DIY superstore. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Ken Morley, Ryan Cartwright, Peter Serafinowicz and Susan Earl

programme.title: Ladies That Punch
programme.desc: Documentary about the Ladies Boxing Association, whose members come from all walks of life, from catwalk models to IT workers

programme.title: The Krays - Mad Axeman (2019)
programme.desc: A gangster is broken out of prison by the Kray twins just before Christmas in 1966. A national manhunt is soon underway to find the fugitive, forcing him to lay low in an East London flat with his minder. Fact-based crime drama, starring Diarmaid Murtagh and Morgan Watkins

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: Triggermen (2002)
programme.desc: Two small-time British con-men are mistaken for professional killers and hired to assassinate a Chicago crime boss - a situation they are quick to exploit. Comedy, starring Donnie Wahlberg, Michael Rapaport and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Miracle in Soho (1957)
programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: Parked (2010)
programme.desc: Fred Daly returns to Ireland with nowhere to live but his car, but he strikes up an unlikely friendship with dope-smoking 21-year-old Cathal when he parks beside him. Encouraged by his young friend's positive attitude to life, Fred meets lonely music teacher Juliana, and these three outsiders are set on a course that will change their lives forever. Drama, starring Colm Meaney, Colin Morgan and Milka Ahlroth

programme.title: Hound (2017)
programme.desc: An alcoholic loner is snapped out of his misery when his faithful dog is murdered by a local gangster. programme.title: Killing Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of two. A blend of documentary, dramatic reconstruction and expert military analysis brings to life the blueprint of Operation Foxley - a 1944 plan to assassinate Adolf Hitler. Featuring testimonies from operatives of Churchill's Special Operations Executive and one of the Fuehrer's most trusted bodyguards, the ethical right to kill an enemy head of state is debated during the unfolding story of LB/X - the man in charge of the plot to kill the Allies' most hated foe

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Susan Earl and Ken Morley

programme.title: Discover London

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: An American woman and a comedian's girlfriend both do their utmost to get into Oxford university to study the works of Lord Byron - but what will they do to gain a place at the great seat of learning? Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Dawn French, Lenny Henry and Nigel Planer

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael tries to get Joe Pesci to star in his proposed new film Bongo in the Congo, but receives an unenthusiastic response. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Spivs (2004)
programme.desc: A professional con-man spends his time honing the skills of his protege and making sure his gang of conniving criminals stays out of trouble. However, when a simple heist backfires the ragtag group are left with a lorry-load of illegal immigrants on their hands - and an angry smuggler with revenge on his mind soon adds to their woes. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott,, Nick Moran and Dominic Monaghan

programme.title: The Returned (2013)
programme.desc: A young doctor goes on the run with her bitten boyfriend and their remaining supplies of a protein used to treat a zombie virus. Sci-fi horror, starring Emily Hampshire, Kris Holden-Ried and Shawn Doyle

programme.title: Perfect Skin (2018)
programme.desc: Katia Matuschak, an 18-year-old Polish woman, embarks on a relationship with mysterious tattoo artist Bob Reid. However, their relationship takes a dark turn as her fascination with him grows, and in an attempt to fulfil his dream, he kidnaps her. Psychological horror, starring Richard Brake, Natalia Kostrzewa and Jo Woodcock

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: The series exploring Britain's criminal underworld returns, beginning with profiles of Liverpool-based gangsters Stephen 'the Devil' French and Boris Boszomenyi

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: West Indies v Sri Lanka. Coverage of the second day of the First Test in the two-match series, taking place at Sir Vivian Richards Stadium, North Sound. This is only the tenth Test Series between the two sides, with Sri Lanka winning three series compared to West Indies two victories. The hosts haven't beaten their opponents in a Test Series since 2003, when they won a two-match series 1-0.

programme.title: Live One-Day International Cricket
programme.desc: India v England. Coverage of the first ODI in the three-match series, which takes place at Maharashtra Cricket Association Stadium in Pune. The last one-day series between the two sides in India saw the home side run out 2-1 winners back in 2016/17. The last time the visitors won a one-day series in India was back in 1984/85, when they comprehensively beat India 4-1 in a five-match series.
